commands.summary: 5/5: isPortAvailable reflects in-use vs free; findAvailablePort returns the preferred port when free and falls back to a free port when taken; restart policy allows up to the budget then denies within the window and recovers after it elapses.
Renderer interaction tests + all node tests (incl. lifecycle) + node --check of main.js/lifecycle.js/etc. green.

commands.command: runtime: occupy 127.0.0.1:5055, launch the app, inspect backend listening port
commands.summary: PR-006 dynamic port: with 5055 held by a dummy listener, the backend detected the conflict and bound a free port (56288) instead of failing.

commands.command: runtime: launch app, kill the backend child process, observe respawn
commands.summary: PR-006 bounded crash-restart: backend on 5055 (pid 18120) was force-killed and the main process restarted it (new pid 12528 on 5055). Intentional stops never restart (current-handle guard); the budget caps auto-restarts at 5/60s and then surfaces a recovery message.

environment.process_note: PR-006 backend lifecycle: port-conflict detection + dynamic-port fallback, bounded crash-restart (5/60s) with a current-handle guard so intentional stops never restart, lifecycle diagnostics in desktop status (effective/configured port, restart count, last exit code), clean shutdown on quit, and a user-facing recovery message in Settings. Lifecycle logic is unit-tested; conflict and crash-restart are runtime-verified.
